channel.title: LLM Timeline — OpenAI Models
channel.description: Large Language Model releases from OpenAI.

item.title: GPT-5.2 Codex (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: GPT-5.2 (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: GPT-5.2 License: open Type: model "GPT‑5.2 sets a new state of the art across many benchmarks, including GDPval, where it outperforms industry professionals at well-specified knowledge work tasks spanning 44 occupations." Announce: https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-2/ MMLU is for Spanish.

item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: GPT-5.1-Codex (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: GPT-5.1 (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: GPT-5.1 License: open Type: model Personality change via fine-tuning. GPQA (no tools) increased from GPT-5=85.7 to GPT-5.1=88.1. MMLU is for Spanish.

item.title: gpt-oss-20b (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: 20.9B License: open Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: GPT-5 (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: GPT-5 License: open Type: model Announce: https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5/. MMLU is based on ES and PT translated from EN.

item.title: gpt-oss-120b (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: gpt-oss-120b License: open Type: model 116.8B total parameters and 5.1B “active” parameters per token per forward pass. https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-oss/

item.title: gpt-oss-20b (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: gpt-oss-20b License: open Type: model 20.9B total and 3.6B active parameters. https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-oss/

item.title: ChatGPT agent (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: codex-mini (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: codex-1 (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: codex-1 License: open Type: model o3 base. "codex-1, a version of OpenAI o3 optimized for software engineering. It was trained using reinforcement learning on real-world coding tasks in a variety of environments to generate code that closely mirrors human style and PR preferences, adheres precisely to instructions, and can iteratively run tests until it receives a passing result."

item.title: gpt-image-1 (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: GPT-4.1 nano (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: o4-mini (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: o4-mini License: open Type: model https://openai.com/index/introducing-o3-and-o4-mini/ MMLU shows a translated LOTE.

item.title: o3 (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: o3 License: open Type: model https://openai.com/index/introducing-o3-and-o4-mini/ MMLU shows a translated LOTE.

item.title: GPT-4.1 (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: GPT-4.1 License: open Type: model Outperforms GPT‑4o "across the board, with major gains in coding and instruction following. They also have larger context windows—supporting up to 1 million tokens of context—and are able to better use that context with improved long-context comprehension. They feature a refreshed knowledge cutoff of June 2024."

item.title: GPT-4o (Mar 2025) (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: 4o Image Generation (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: o1-pro (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: GPT-4.5 (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: GPT-4.5 (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: GPT-4.5 License: open Type: model "Our largest and best model for chat" https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-4-5/ "GPT-4.5 is not a frontier model, but it is OpenAI’s largest LLM, improving on GPT-4’s computational efficiency by more than 10x. While GPT-4.5 demonstrates increased world knowledge, improved writing ability, and refined personality over previous models, it does not introduce net-new frontier capabilities compared to previous reasoning releases, and its performance is below that of o1, o3-mini, and deep research on most preparedness evaluations."

item.title: o3-mini (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: GPT-4o (Jan 2025) (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: Computer-Using Agent (CUA) (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: o3-mini (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: o3-mini License: open Type: model GPQA=79.7 for 'high' thinking. ALPrompt 2025H1=1/5. My analysis is that this model’s performance is very poor, with responses often becoming disordered and illogical. OpenAI compared o3-mini to OpenAI’s software engineers, and it performed very poorly (o3-mini=0%, o1=12%). "o3-mini models have the lowest performance, with scores of 0%… We suspect o3-mini’s low performance is due to poor instruction following and confusion about specifying tools in the correct format. The model often attempts to use a hallucinated bash tool rather than python despite constant, multi-shot prompting and feedback that this format is incorrect. This resulted in long conversations that likely hurt its performance." (o3-mini paper, p31)

item.title: GPT-4b (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: GPT-4b License: closed Type: model Protein sequence model. "The model was trained on examples of protein sequences from many species, as well as information on which proteins tend to interact with one another. While that’s a lot of data, it’s just a fraction of what OpenAI’s flagship chatbots were trained on, making GPT-4b an example of a “small language model” that works with a focused data set." https://www.technologyreview.com/2025/01/17/1110086/openai-has-created-an-ai-model-for-longevity-science/

item.title: o3 (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: o1 (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: o3-preview (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: o3-preview License: open Type: model SoTA model for Dec/2024. Parameter estimate is very rough centrepoint for range 400B-52T.

item.title: o1-2024-12-17 (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: o1-2024-12-17 License: closed Type: model "o1-2024-12-17 sets new state-of-the-art results on several benchmarks, improving cost-efficiency and performance."

item.title: o1 (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: o1 License: open Type: model "a version of our most intelligent model that thinks longer for the most reliable responses" System card about safety only: https://cdn.openai.com/o1-system-card-20241205.pdf

item.title: GPT-4o (Nov 2024) (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: gpt-4o-2024-11-20 (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: gpt-4o-2024-11-20 License: open Type: model Material decrease in benchmark scores (GPQA: -13.37%, MMLU: -3.38%) compared to Aug/2024. Pruned? Quantized? https://github.com/openai/simple-evals

item.title: o1-mini (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.description: Parameters: o1-preview License: closed Type: model

item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: SearchGPT (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: GPT-4o mini (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: GPT-4o mini (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: GPT-4o mini License: open Type: model Omnimodel. "OpenAI would not disclose exactly how large GPT-4o mini is, but said it’s roughly in the same tier as other small AI models, such as Llama 3 8b, Claude Haiku and Gemini 1.5 Flash." https://techcrunch.com/2024/07/18/openai-unveils-gpt-4o-mini-a-small-ai-model-powering-chatgpt/ "tested GPT-4o to identify potential risks, which we have addressed and plan to share the details of in the forthcoming GPT-4o system card and Preparedness scorecard." And related paper about instruction hierarchy: https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.13208

item.title: CriticGPT (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: CriticGPT (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: CriticGPT License: closed Type: model "LLM Critics Help Catch LLM Bugs" Announce: https://openai.com/index/finding-gpt4s-mistakes-with-gpt-4/

item.title: GPT-4o (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: GPT-4o (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: GPT-4o License: closed Type: model gpt-4o-2024-05-13 no longer easily available, so hidden in the Model Table rankings. Omnimodel. ‘[GPT-4o is] likely an early checkpoint of GPT-5’. https://twitter.com/drjimfan/status/1790089671365767313 ELO: https://twitter.com/LiamFedus/status/1790064963966370209 Demo: https://youtu.be/DQacCB9tDaw

item.title: GPT-4 Turbo (Apr 2024) (OpenAI)
item.description: License: closed Type: model AI model by OpenAI

item.title: gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09 (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09 License: open Type: model This is such a significantly better model that I've added it here. This GPQA=46.5%, old GPT-4 GPQA=36%. https://twitter.com/EpochAIResearch/status/1778463039932584205 MMLU scores are unclear, but may have improved by 1%: https://twitter.com/OpenAI/status/1778602770784002136. Final benchmarks are here: https://archive.md/6Cc0Z

item.title: GPT-1 (OpenAI)
item.description: Parameters: GPT-1 License: open Type: model "GPT-1 — 984 M tokens corpus × 100 epochs × 1 token per word → 98.4 B tokens processed" Books only. "We train for 100 epochs on minibatches of 64 randomly sampled, contiguous sequences of 512 tokens." =3,276,800
